course_title: Impact Academy 2: Designing Solutions that Create Value

learning_outcomes.description: Working individually or in teams, identify and critically evaluate the desirability, feasibility, and viability of a value creation opportunity by applying concepts, tools, and techniques and gathering supporting data.
Critically evaluate one’s own suitability and willingness to execute a value creation opportunity in light of new insights and data sensemaking.
Convey cogent value creation strategies developed through synthesis of and adaptation to stakeholder feedback and insights.

assessments.task_description:
You will pitch your idea (max 15 min, followed by Q&A) by analysing it against the backdrop of the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course including any initial validation and supporting evidence. Each student will present in class (given confidentially permits) and will respond to questions from the audience.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: One member per team has to submit the slides via Blackboard BEFORE the pitch.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Entry Reflection 27/07/2026 5:30 pm

Progress Reflection 1 24/08/2026 1:00 pm

Progress Reflection 2 21/09/2026 1:00 pm

Progress Reflection 3 19/10/2026 1:00 pm

Exit Reflection 9/11/2026 5:30 pm
assessments.task_description: Throughout the course, you will keep a reflective journal of your opportunity journey. Five times throughout the course, you will reflect on the progress that you make and the successes and challenges you faced while developing your opportunities based on the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course. Individual reflections should be approximately 300 words / 3–4 minutes video and include entry and exit reflections.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit your reflections individually via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will deliver a max 7 min investor pitch in video format that demonstrates the opportunity is viable, feasible, and desirable.

The video is accompanied by an explanation summarising the logic and evidence underlying the proposed solution and how it was informed by the insights gained throughout the course. Submission of the explanation can be in video (20-30 minutes with slide deck) or written format (20 pages).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
